title: Multiparameter quantum estimation and entanglement in top--antitop quark production
authors: Omar Bachain, Elhabib Jaloum, Mohamed Amazioug, Rachid Ahl Laamara
abstract: We investigate the interplay between quantum correlations and multiparameter quantum estimation in top--antitop quark pair production through the gluon-fusion channel. Using the spin density matrix formalism, we construct an effective two-qubit quantum state governed by the relativistic parameters associated with the scattering process. Within the framework of quantum metrology, we derive the quantum Fisher information matrix for the simultaneous estimation of the relativistic velocity parameter and the production angle, and we analyze the corresponding quantum precision bounds. Our results reveal highly nontrivial estimation regimes strongly controlled by relativistic spin correlations and scattering geometry. We further characterize the produced state through the concurrence and demonstrate the existence of strong connections between entanglement structures and multiparameter estimation sensitivity. Finally, we discuss the experimental feasibility of probing these effects at the Large Hadron Collider through spin-correlation observables and reconstructed top--antitop density matrices. Our results identify top--antitop production as a unique relativistic platform for exploring quantum information theory and multiparameter quantum metrology in high-energy physics.
summary: This theoretical study explores how quantum entanglement in top-antitop quark production can enhance the precision of measuring kinematic parameters like velocity and production angle. By calculating the Quantum Fisher Information Matrix and the concurrence, the authors demonstrate a direct link: strong quantum correlations signal regimes where quantum metrology offers superior measurement capabilities compared to classical methods.
main_problem: The paper investigates the interplay between quantum correlations (entanglement) and the precision limits achievable in simultaneously estimating multiple physical parameters ($ eta$ and $\Theta$) during top-antitop quark pair production.
main_result: The simultaneous estimation strategy can yield a significant metrological advantage over individual estimations, and this advantage is strongly correlated with the degree of quantum entanglement present in the produced state.
method: The authors utilized the spin density matrix formalism to model the system, deriving the Quantum Fisher Information Matrix (QFIM) to set the ultimate precision bounds, alongside calculating the concurrence to quantify entanglement.
model_or_system: The physical system is top-antitop quark pair production ($g+g 	o t ar{t}$) at high-energy colliders, modeled effectively as a bipartite spin-1/2 quantum state governed by relativistic parameters like velocity ($ eta$) and production angle ($\Theta$).
key_observables: Quantum Fisher Information Matrix (QFIM), Concurrence, Estimation Variances ($\Delta eta)^2, (\Delta\Theta)^2$, and the metrological advantage ratio $\Gamma(\Theta,  eta)$.
important_parameters: Relativistic velocity ($ eta$), production angle ($\Theta$), and the ultrarelativistic limit ($ eta 	o 1$).
main_assumptions: The analysis is performed at leading order in QCD, and the effective two-qubit description is valid due to the top quark's short lifetime.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the dependence of estimation variances on $\Theta$ and $ eta$, showing optimal regimes, and plot the Concurrence versus $\Theta$ and $ eta$, demonstrating entanglement revival in the ultrarelativistic limit.
paper_structure: The paper systematically derives the quantum state using spin density matrices, calculates the QFIM for simultaneous estimation, compares this to individual estimations using the ratio $\Gamma$, and finally correlates the resulting metrological advantage with the calculated entanglement (concurrence).
why_interesting: While the application is in particle physics, the core methodology—using quantum information measures (entanglement, QFIM) to enhance parameter estimation beyond classical limits—is directly applicable to quantum sensing and metrology problems in AMO physics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

Fig. 4: Ratio Γ(Θ, β) between the simultaneous- and individual-estimation variances associated with the relativistic parameters β and Θ. (a) Γ(Θ, β) as a function of Θ for different values of β. (b) Γ(Θ, β) as a function of β for different values of Θ.

Fig. 5: Concurrence C of the produced top–antitop quantum state. (a) C as a function of the production angle Θ for different values of the relativistic parameter β. (b) C as a function of β for different values of the production angle Θ.
